##NAMES=Natropalermoite
##RRUFFID=R130092
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_2_SrAl_4_(PO_4_)_4_(OH)_4_
##LOCALITY=Palermo No. 1 mine, Groton, New Hampshire, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Benjamin N. Schumer
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless, prismatic, striated, elongated along [100], visually indistinguishable from palermoite (R120141). Associated with grey to colorless bladed eosphorite, groups of acicular childrenite, quartz, goethite and lefontite R140539. Type sample
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130092
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_1.70_Li_0.30_)_2.00_(Sr_0.96_Ca_0.02_Ba_0.01_)(Al_3.83_Mg_0.04_Mn_0.03_Fe_0.03_)(PO_4_)_4.06_(OH)_4.00_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated

##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
